Source   Edit  

Fleet movement, colonization, and seek home operations

This module handles all fleet order resolution including:

  • Fleet movement with pathfinding and lane traversal rules
  • Colonization orders and new colony establishment
  • Automated Seek Home behavior for stranded fleets
  • Helper functions for path finding and hostility detection

Imports

../../common/hex, ../../common/types/core, ../../common/types/combat, ../../common/types/units, ../gamestate, ../orders, ../fleet, ../starmap, ../spacelift, ../colonization/engine, ../diplomacy/types, ../config/prestige_config, ../config/population_config, ../prestige, types

Procs

proc autoLoadCargo(state: var GameState; orders: Table[HouseId, OrderPacket];
                   events: var seq[GameEvent]) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Automatically load available marines/colonists onto empty transports at colonies Only auto-load if no manual cargo order exists for that fleet Source   Edit   
proc estimatePathRisk(state: GameState; path: seq[SystemId]; houseId: HouseId): int {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Estimate risk level of a path (0 = safe, higher = more risky) Uses fog-of-war information available to the house Source   Edit   
proc findClosestOwnedColony(state: GameState; fromSystem: SystemId;
                            houseId: HouseId): Option[SystemId] {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Find the closest owned colony for a house, excluding the fromSystem Returns None if house has no colonies Used by Space Guild to find alternative delivery destination Also used for automated Seek Home behavior for stranded fleets

INTEGRATION: Checks house's pre-planned fallback routes first for optimal retreat paths

Source   Edit   
proc isSystemHostile(state: GameState; systemId: SystemId; houseId: HouseId): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if a system is hostile to a house based on known intel (fog-of-war) System is hostile if player KNOWS it contains:
  1. Enemy colony (from intelligence database or visibility)

2. Enemy fleets (from intelligence database or visibility) IMPORTANT: This respects fog-of-war - only uses information available to the house

Source   Edit   
proc resolveColonizationOrder(state: var GameState; houseId: HouseId;
                              order: FleetOrder; events: var seq[GameEvent]) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Establish a new colony with prestige rewards Source   Edit   
proc resolveMovementOrder(state: var GameState; houseId: HouseId;
                          order: FleetOrder; events: var seq[GameEvent]) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Execute a fleet movement order with pathfinding and lane traversal rules Per operations.md:6.1 - Lane traversal rules:
  • Major lanes: 2 jumps per turn if all systems owned by player
  • Major lanes: 1 jump per turn if jumping into unexplored/rival system
  • Minor/Restricted lanes: 1 jump per turn maximum
  • Crippled ships or Spacelift ships cannot cross Restricted lanes
Source   Edit   
proc shouldAutoSeekHome(state: GameState; fleet: Fleet; order: FleetOrder): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Determine if a fleet should automatically seek home due to dangerous situation Respects house's auto-retreat policy setting Triggers based on policy:
  • Never: Never auto-retreat
  • MissionsOnly: Abort missions (ETAC, Guard, Blockade) when target lost
  • ConservativeLosing: Also retreat fleets clearly losing combat
  • AggressiveSurvival: Also retreat any fleet at risk
Source   Edit
